(1815–1899) was a Christian [starch](/wiki/Starch "Starch") manufacturer in London and owner of [Samuel Berger & Co.](/w/index.php?title=Samuel_Berger_%26_Co.&action=edit&redlink=1 "Samuel Berger & Co. (page does not exist)"), a patent rice starch manufacturer, who became the first home (England) director of the [China Inland Mission](/wiki/China_Inland_Mission "China Inland Mission") with [James Hudson Taylor](/wiki/James_Hudson_Taylor "James Hudson Taylor") in 1865. At this time the headquarters of the mission agency was located at [Saint Hill Manor](/wiki/Saint_Hill_Manor "Saint Hill Manor") in [East Grinstead](/wiki/East_Grinstead "East Grinstead"), England. As the Home Director he was responsible for editing the *Occasional Paper of the China Inland Mission* and carrying on the work of sending more [missionaries](/wiki/Missionaries "Missionaries") to follow Hudson Taylor to China.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Christian manufacturer

| William Thomas Berger | |
| --- | --- |
| Missionary Director and Rice Starch Manufacturer | |
| Born | 1815 (1815)  England |
| Died | 9 January 1899(1899-01-09) (aged 83–84)  Cannes, France |

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/d/d9/William_%26_Mary_Berger.jpg/250px-William_%26_Mary_Berger.jpg)](/wiki/File:William_%26_Mary_Berger.jpg)

William and Mary Berger.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/32/Occasional_Paper_1875.jpg/250px-Occasional_Paper_1875.jpg)](/wiki/File:Occasional_Paper_1875.jpg)

Cover of the *Occasional Paper of the China Inland Mission* in 1875.

**William Thomas Berger** (1815–1899) was a Christian [starch](/wiki/Starch "Starch") manufacturer in London and owner of [Samuel Berger & Co.](/w/index.php?title=Samuel_Berger_%26_Co.&action=edit&redlink=1 "Samuel Berger & Co. (page does not exist)"), a patent rice starch manufacturer, who became the first home (England) director of the [China Inland Mission](/wiki/China_Inland_Mission "China Inland Mission") with [James Hudson Taylor](/wiki/James_Hudson_Taylor "James Hudson Taylor") in 1865. At this time the headquarters of the mission agency was located at [Saint Hill Manor](/wiki/Saint_Hill_Manor "Saint Hill Manor") in [East Grinstead](/wiki/East_Grinstead "East Grinstead"), England. As the Home Director he was responsible for editing the *Occasional Paper of the China Inland Mission* and carrying on the work of sending more [missionaries](/wiki/Missionaries "Missionaries") to follow Hudson Taylor to China.

At the time of the [Yangzhou riot](/wiki/Yangzhou_riot "Yangzhou riot") that brought unwelcome notoriety to the mission activity in China, Berger had to defend Taylor and his group from the attacks of the British press. Often, he had to assume this role with little or no knowledge of the current events in China due to the delay in communication with the missionaries overseas.

The Bergers resigned due to failing health and also due to his personal convictions which were similar to [Andrew Jukes](/wiki/Andrew_Jukes_(theologian) "Andrew Jukes (theologian)") (in conflict with the traditional Christian principles of the China Inland Mission) regarding the eternal punishment of non-Christians. He remained a faithful supporter of the mission and a friend of Hudson Taylor until his death.
